# Source Catalog - backlog-structuring

This catalog tracks the authoritative external sources used to keep this skill current and reusable.

| Source | What It Provides | Why It Is Valuable | In Use | Last Evaluated | Current Status | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| [Scrum Guide](https://www.scrumguides.org/scrum-guide.html) | Canonical Scrum artifacts, accountabilities, goals, and increment model | Provides the cleanest public reference for backlog, sprint, and increment structure | Yes | 2026-04-24 | Current | Used for goal, backlog, and increment hierarchy framing |
| [Define features and epics to organize your backlog](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/devops/boards/backlogs/define-features-epics?view=azure-devops) | Practical backlog hierarchy and rollup examples | Turns abstract hierarchy advice into concrete portfolio-to-story structuring patterns | Yes | 2026-04-24 | Current | Used for hierarchy normalization and rollup thinking |
| [User Stories](https://www.atlassian.com/agile/project-management/user-stories) | User-story framing and confirmation patterns | Useful for preserving user-value language at the correct backlog level | Yes | 2026-04-24 | Current | Used as a secondary source for value-oriented item writing |

## Freshness Policy

- Default maximum age: 30 days.
- If Last Evaluated exceeds the threshold, set Current Status to Needs Review.
- If a source is no longer maintained or relevant, mark In Use as No and Current Status as Deprecated.
